compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can attest worktree gate results without re-running tests or review
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Define a stable attestation format contract for test-gate-status and review-status files, and add an --attest mode to record-test-status.sh and record-review.sh that writes gate status with a new diff hash without re-running tests or re-validating review findings.
**Why**: harvest-worktree.sh needs to re-record gate status files with a post-merge diff hash. The existing recording scripts always re-run tests or re-validate findings. An attestation mode allows verified trust transfer from a worktree that already passed its gates.
**Scope**:
- IN: Document the test-gate-status and review-status file format as a contract; add --attest <worktree-artifacts-dir> flag to record-test-status.sh that verifies the source status is passing and writes a new status with the current diff hash and the union of source tested_files + locally-required tested_files; add --attest <worktree-artifacts-dir> flag to record-review.sh that verifies the source review passed and writes a new review-status with the current diff hash (skipping the overlap check); both scripts must verify the source worktree's status exists and is passing before attesting
- OUT: Changes to pre-commit hook validation logic; changes to compute-diff-hash.sh; harvest-worktree.sh itself (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, record-test-status.sh --attest <worktree-artifacts-dir> writes test-gate-status with the current diff hash, status=passed, and tested_files populated from the union of the source worktree's tested_files and locally-required tests for session-side staged files
  ← Satisfies: "When the orchestrator runs harvest-worktree.sh, a clean worktree merge completes in a single invocation"
- When this story is complete, record-review.sh --attest <worktree-artifacts-dir> writes review-status with the current diff hash, status=passed, and the source worktree's reviewer_hash and score, without running the findings-file overlap check
  ← Satisfies: "When the orchestrator runs harvest-worktree.sh, a clean worktree merge completes in a single invocation"
- When this story is complete, both --attest modes refuse to write if the source worktree's gate status is missing, failed, or has a stale diff hash (does not match the worktree branch HEAD content)
  ← Satisfies: "When a worktree branch's test-gate-status or review-status is missing or failed, harvest-worktree.sh refuses to merge and exits non-zero"
- When this story is complete, the attested test-gate-status and review-status files include an attest_source field containing the source worktree ID
  ← Satisfies: "attestation includes the source worktree ID so the trust chain is auditable"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] compute-diff-hash.sh cache keyed on mtime+size — same-second staging may produce stale cache hits on HFS+. Attestation should compute hash only once, after all staging is complete.
- [Maintainability] Document the test-gate-status and review-status file format as a contract so harvest-worktree.sh and pre-commit hooks stay in sync if the format evolves.
- [Reliability] Stale worktree status: --attest must verify the source test-gate-status diff_hash is consistent with the worktree branch's content, not just check status=passed.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
